summary: The law governing an operating unit is set by where that unit operates, not by where its parent account is registered: effective jurisdiction resolves most-specific-first from the unit to the account default, and a jurisdiction is a versioned policy bundle held as data whose already-emitted output stays pinned to the version in force when it was produced.

rules.rule: Resolve effective jurisdiction as coalesce(the operating unit's own jurisdiction, the account's default jurisdiction) — most specific wins, absent levels inherit upward. A repo whose operating units cannot span jurisdictions declares N-A(single-jurisdiction) instead, or declares ADOPTED-NATIVE per §2 where it already resolves a law-bearing attribute below the account by another mechanism.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Bind jurisdiction at the account level as the only level when operating units within that account span jurisdictions. (A repo whose units cannot span them is not in violation — see the §2 landing.)

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Hold a jurisdiction as a versioned bundle of rules-as-data with an explicit validity window; a normative change ships as a new version bearing its own start date and the system switches on that date, never earlier and never retroactively.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Re-derive or rewrite already-emitted output under a later bundle version — emitted output is pinned to the version in force when it was produced.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Collapse the territory data-catalogue (a value such as a person's nationality) and the legal jurisdiction governing an operating unit into one field — they are separate concerns.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
